# Persisted-State & Migration Requirements Checklist: GUI task fidelity

**Purpose**: Validate that the requirements governing the persisted-schedule
change, its forward migration, and the shared client contract are complete,
unambiguous, and objectively verifiable — before any of it is built. This is the
gating checklist: the constitution names forward-only non-destructive store
migrations a safety-critical surface (CLAUDE.md, Non-negotiables).
**Created**: 2026-07-22
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

**Depth**: Formal release gate · **Audience**: Reviewer (PR) · **Focus**:
persisted-state mutation, migration safety, shared client contract

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are requirements defined for what happens to the retained phrase when a schedule is replaced rather than created? [Gap, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK002 Is the requirement that pre-existing schedules survive the upgrade stated as an observable outcome rather than a schema property? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, §SC-007]
- [x] CHK003 Are requirements defined for the case where a derived phrase cannot be produced at all? [Coverage, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK004 Does the spec state whether derivation happens once at upgrade time or on every read? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK005 Are rollback or recovery requirements defined for a migration that fails partway? [Gap, Exception Flow]
- [ ] CHK006 Are requirements defined for re-running the upgrade against an already-upgraded installation? [Coverage, Gap]
- [x] CHK007 Is the set of recurrence forms a phrase must be derivable from explicitly bounded? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK008 Are requirements defined for one-off schedules, which retain no phrase? [Coverage, Spec §Assumptions]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK009 Is "equivalent phrase" (FR-003) defined precisely enough to be objectively judged? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003]
- [x] CHK010 Is "describes the same recurrence" (FR-004) expressed as a verifiable identity rather than a subjective similarity? [Measurability, Spec §FR-004]
- [x] CHK011 Is the prohibition on deriving an anchor stated with its reason, so a later reader cannot mistake it for an oversight? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK012 Are the three distinct group-update intents named unambiguously, with the omitted case explicitly mapped? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014]
- [x] CHK013 Is "forward-only and non-destructive" (FR-002) reducible to a checkable property? [Measurability, Spec §FR-002]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK014 Does the inertness requirement for the phrase (FR-011a) conflict with any requirement that a client re-submits it? [Consistency, Spec §FR-011a, §FR-006]
- [x] CHK015 Do the phrase, the plain-language summary, and the recurrence have three distinct, non-overlapping stated roles? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK016 Is the CLI's existing "omitted means unchanged" behavior stated consistently with the new three-state model? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014, §FR-015]
- [x] CHK017 Do the timezone re-interpretation requirement (FR-011) and the "untouched save changes nothing" requirement (FR-008) coexist without contradiction? [Conflict, Spec §FR-008, §FR-011]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK018 Can "zero upcoming run times change" (SC-007) be measured without inspecting storage internals? [Measurability, Spec §SC-007]
- [x] CHK019 Is there an acceptance criterion that fails if a derived phrase round-trips to a different recurrence? [Traceability, Spec §FR-004]
- [x] CHK020 Do the requirements state that each fixed defect needs a test failing before the fix? [Completeness, Spec §FR-023]
- [x] CHK021 Is the migration-survival requirement backed by its own named test obligation? [Traceability, Spec §FR-024]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK022 Are requirements defined for a schedule whose recurrence falls outside the phrase vocabulary? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK023 Are requirements defined for a one-off whose stored instant is already in the past? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK024 Are requirements defined for assigning a task to a group that ceased to exist between reading and writing? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-016]
- [x] CHK025 Are requirements defined for an unresolvable stored group reference? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-019a]
- [x] CHK026 Are requirements defined for a task edited or deleted concurrently with an open editor? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK027 Is the assumption that the phrase vocabulary is unchanged by this feature stated explicitly? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK028 Is the assumption that group-deletion semantics are unchanged stated and traceable to existing behavior? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK029 Is the dependency on the existing live-update mechanism documented rather than assumed? [Dependency, Spec §Assumptions, §FR-022]
- [x] CHK030 Is the assumption that GUI and CLI share one boundary — so a GUI capability necessarily reaches the CLI — recorded? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, §FR-015]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
- An unchecked item at `/speckit-analyze` time is a finding, not a deferral
